description: Two live `ggen sync run` invocations against the same ggen.toml/ontology.ttl produce byte-identical output inventories. Proven twice: (1) directly against the pristine, as-shipped pack templates (no modification), and (2) again after an unrelated template fix (see FRONTMATTER_LEAK finding referenced by evidence/consumer-replacement.json) was applied, for consistency. In both cases two consecutive runs matched exactly.
commands: ggen sync run --format json   # run 2, no other changes in between
find <14 canonical output paths> | sort | xargs shasum -a 256 | shasum -a 256   # inventory digest

runs.label: as-shipped pack (pristine templates, frontmatter-leak bug still present)
post-frontmatter-fix templates (used for cap11/cap12; canonical 14-path file list)

note: This capability's falsifier concerns digest reproducibility of the sync pipeline, not correctness of the generated code, so it holds even against the pristine pack that (separately) fails to compile -- see evidence/consumer-replacement.json for that unrelated, real defect.
